reply:
[[BLOCK:srt_49]]
Speaker 2: I earned my graduate degree from Golden Gate University.

[[BLOCK:srt_50]]
Speaker 2: Another example is Bernard Tyson, who served as chairman of Kaiser Health, a major healthcare organization headquartered in the Bay Area.

[[BLOCK:srt_51]]
Speaker 2: Bernard was widely respected not only for his exceptional leadership as a CEO, but also for his deep commitment to community building and public service.

[[BLOCK:srt_52]]
Speaker 2: He truly embodied our philosophy: achieving business success while creating meaningful value for society.

[[BLOCK:srt_53]]
Speaker 1: I’m reminded of another example. Solomon Darwin is now a professor at the Berkeley Haas School of Business. He earned his MBA at Golden Gate University.

[[BLOCK:srt_54]]
Speaker 1: Before that, he worked as a janitor.

[[BLOCK:srt_55]]
Speaker 1: When other universities failed to see his potential, a professor at Golden Gate University recognized something remarkable in him. We admitted him and nurtured his talent. He went on to become a highly respected professor at Berkeley and a leading advocate of the “Smart Village” movement.

[[BLOCK:srt_56]]
Speaker 1: That’s the spirit of our institution—discovering talent, recognizing potential, and investing in people’s growth. Even when others overlook them, we’re willing to give them the opportunity to thrive.

[[BLOCK:srt_57]]
Speaker 3: Wonderful—that’s truly inspiring.

[[BLOCK:srt_58]]
Speaker 3: Golden Gate University was one of the first institutions in the United States to offer online education.

[[BLOCK:srt_59]]
Speaker 3: Could you tell us how Golden Gate University’s online programs first got started and what major milestones you’ve reached so far?

[[BLOCK:srt_60]]
Speaker 1: Many people don’t realize that Golden Gate University was among the very first universities to offer online degree programs. We began experimenting with online education back when computer screens displayed only text.
